reviews.review_msg: Great professor but really makes you work for a good mark. Prepare to practice IPA symbols and syntax trees until you've drilled them into your brain device

His slides are too hard to follow - literally paragraphs. There is ALOT of material in this course but we only got tested on random select topics. There is are 2 midterms (one for each textbook), a term paper, and a final presentation. The presentation is recorded and marked very harshly. Good luck if you take a class with him..

Cliff is a tough grader but it is evident he really cares about his students. Make sure you pay attention and ask questions! He is very friendly one-on-one so don't be afraid to see him during his office hours.

Cliff is a great professor. He's organized on canvas, records all of his lectures incase you miss one, and is readily accessible for help outside of class. I do think that visiting him in his office hours are extremely beneficial, especially for a writing class.

The course is too painful. What cliff does is more or less what we call a destruction of humanity. I went to every single office hr to get help but he seems to see no improvement of you. It is a course to help you improve your writing skills, but Cliff used it to destroy your self-esteem on writing academic papers. Thank you for your help btw!

if you like public speaking/want to be an esl teacher, you should take LING 362 with him (in fact, he is the only one who teaches LING 362 at sfu). he is definitely a tough grader and you would get your points deducted even for the very tiny things. so, MAKE SURE to follow his instructions, visit his office hours, and put as much effort as you can

This is a great class that teaches valuable public speaking, and presentation skills. Professor gives specific feedback, marks harshly. Do something wrong and will be penalized. Sometimes intimidating. His lecture slides are basically his speech ( good/ bad?).  But each presentation is 2030% of your grade. DO NOT go over time. PRACTICE A LOT.
Don't take this course with this professor if you want to boost your GPA

Lectures were long but he's super engaging. Quizzes were pretty nasty, but tests were WAY worse, like they really did batter me. Online quizzes were decent but super specific. He does a great job of explaining thing, is very passionate about ling, and goes over quizzes/tests well. Overall decent class, just pls pls pls be prepared for testing.

I'm a ling major and I took this class in my last semester for an LD credit I needed.  Much of Cliff's theories seemed incorrect. I was taught very contradictory things in this class than I had learned in 200, and 300 level classes. Go to class and memorize his opinion and you'll do fine, but expect to learn drastically different things elsewhere.

Cliff is a great prof! His passion for linguistics is clear in his interactive lectures. He is very caring and he makes each class interesting. However, this is not a "gpa booster" class. He is a fair but tough marker - you need a thorough understanding of the material to do well. Would recommend if you're passionate about linguistics.
so hard....

Engaging, funny and caring. He gives you everything you need to pass, responds to e-mails and will always help you in office hours. He gives you everything you need to get a good grade but you have to work for it

Took this class as it sounded very interesting. Was excited to learn, but by exam week I was regretting taking this course. Slides were very informative, tests looked like they were from a 400 level course. Don't take this class if you're looking for an elective. If you arent in Linguistics (I am a bus student) then stay away.

Was not looking forward to this class since it was 3 hours but he has made things so interesting right from the beginning! Lectures are super clear and slides are easy to understand. Quizzes are reasonable, tests are a bit tough but nothing out of the ordinary. His review assignments and slides are great. Overall, will do his best to help you pass
dont take it. his exam is harder than other teachers who have ever tought ling200
dont have to go to class. I learn by myself, the score was even higher than go to lec. study at home is more efficiency. dont have to look at book just the PPT.

He seems tough and unfriendly but if you are willing to stop by his office hours often, you will find him helpful. This course does not require too much of work in general. English is not my first language and I did not study much for the MT, still I got a B-. Now I am able to speak in front of the class. I love it!

Really recommend this class. Wish I'd taken the grammar class with him too. He records lectures so it's really helpful for review. Also gives midterms back; great for review. Best prof I had this semester. Makes the long lectures less boring too. Also has a lot of office hours for help. Will make sure everything is fair for his students.

He is really a good professor, but his exams are not easy. You don't need to attend his class every time cause he posted videos on canvas. Try to organize his notes and ask him when you get confused, you can get good marks.
Really good at getting the material across. Just don't take if you're looking for an easy credit. For LING362 he expects a lot out of you.
Really great- he explained everything clearly, was very helpful answering questions, and made some of the more boring material interesting.

He was simply fantastic. He was engaging with the class, deeply interested in the material and offered continual help. The subject matter wasn't for everyone but I have taken what he taught into real life context. It's a hard class and there is a lot of material but i thoroughly valued my experience with him.

This prof loves the material. His multiple choice questions are very hard and you have to study a lot for this class. If you do not want to go on with linguistics I wouldnt recommend this class. There are other ones which are easier...
Hardest final exam i've ever taken so far at SFU
not respected students' work

Hardest multiple choice exams imaginable. Lots of material that goes off in all directions, including the way the mouth makes sounds, and the symbols for those sounds. It's a hodgepodge of all kinds of stuff, and memorizing the material won't cut it. You need to understand it and apply it in the exam. Very tough. You've been warned.

THE most boring class I've ever taken, mostly because of the prof but the lecture was 3 hours long on Monday... The actual material was interesting, but I would suggest looking it up on your own time rather than wasting money. The worst part was the final exam, which tested your ability to work out word problems over your knowledge of the material.
hard to get a good grade on his exams, all MC questions.

Overall, the course is easy and his exams are fair. Lectures can sometimes get boring because he can go on about topics that are unrelated to the lecture but are related to Linguistics. He makes "dad" or "old man" jokes that can sometimes be funny but can also be received as lame by some.

Although the class is whooping 3-hours long is an really easy credit. It requires memorization but everything that he tests you on exams are basically similar questions from the weekly quizzes. It can be boring, but he does try his best to make the class interesting. My savior for the class was my merriam-webster's dictionary app! invest in it!

If you want to get a higher grade in this course. Please choose other instructors. His final is hard and you have to read the whole textbook so than you will not get a bad grade. His lecture is useless and all you can do is to practice a lot.

I took this class because I needed one more lower division to graduate. I love linguistics and I am a constant B to B+ student in Linguistic. I have taken upper division classes in Linguistics that are easier than this. Its not the content that is difficult but his exams are way more difficult than they need to be.
Attend Lectures/Listen. Read Notes and use textbook where the lecture notes cover. You will do fine. People who complain obviously do not study. Overall, this class is not too bad

Good sense of humour even if not all of his jokes fly. Took LING 110 with him as well, got a fantastic grade but his exams  are very difficult. His classes are not easy but if you do the work, you should do fine. No cakewalk. On the upside, he always offers extra office hours and is very approachable during that time.

Very bad notes he made for students. You cannot catch up with the lecture content without reading the text book throughly. He cannot give clear rules and answers for his questions on his slides. Not recommend this course if you want to have high GPA or don't have enough time for this course.
Avoid taking any LING courses with him, take only if you want a real challenge. My advice, DON'T :)

You have to read the whole textbook in order to get a good mark. The 1st MT was hard but the 2nd one became easier. I got a D on MT1, an A- on MT2, and a B on the final. THIS COURSE IS NOT A GPA BOOSTER.

There is no problem with the prof, its the content of this course may be very dry and tricky for people with out any knowledge of how words are formed. Not a GPA booster definitely, but if you have a high level of attention to detail, than go for it

He is the hardest prof ever in linguistics department. His exams were really hard, you must understand all the materials prior to class, otherwise, you will have no ideas what hes talking about in class.

He is a horrible lecturer! blabbers on and on about nothing in class pretending to make jokes and then makes the MT's super hard and about nothing that you learnt or was in the book. come on! this is a 100 level course. he needs to stop thinking its the most important thing in the world.

He is very passionate about ling but this is a 100 level class we do not need this amount useless info. His MT are so head i just sat there and failed, his finals are okay but still very difficult. IF you are looking for a GPA booster this is not the professor you want.
good prof.. very funny... quizzes are okay.. hard exams though, but he does do review.. not a bad prof, you can tell he cares

Very knowledgeable in the material of LING110. Has his own sense of humor, but kind of dry lecture! MT questions are hard. Has preference on those hard ones. Class avg. improves on 2nd MT. Enjoy his Word-of-the-day and learn some cool words! Overall, I like this prof, but I hope he can cut down some useless & confusing lines in lecture slides.

The Professor has great passion for the course.But neither his lecture is well organized nor the slides context are expressed clearly. The lecture slides are too detailed and complicated. If you are an International student from China as me and seeking a GPA Booster, you would better not choose this professor in ling110.
His lecture notes were difficult to understand, but the contents were not that hard. If you read the textbooks you will be fine. HOWEVER I do not recommend anyone to take this class with him.

I noticed he can be really rude when speaking with students including myself. Not a good trait for someone who is supposed to be teaching. He blabbers but not enough clear info, and he makes frequent mitakes which is ok to an extent but not that much.

His class is easy....if you do the work. He is pleasant....if you pay attention and show respect. Basically, you get from it what you put into it, simple as that. He is definitely one of my favourite professors!
A very hard marker and not one to truly accept feedback or criticism on his part. He makes things harder than they need to be. Take a course of his if you are willing to pour in plenty of effort.

Only take his classes if you are taking school seriously. He is a tough prof and marks are not easy to get. Attending class is a MUST to get a decent mark. 3 hour lecture but he tries to break it up for you with group discussions and administrative stuff to mix it up. I took Ling 200 with him before.

He is a horrible teacher. I've had four classes with him so far. He is rude and has a cheesy and boring sense of humour. His exams are extremely hard for the courses he teaches (i've had a 100 class with him before.) Don't take his class if you can help it.

Come on man! This is just a 100 level class and he makes it likes a 400 level one. Mid- term is extremely tough, final seems better but it's still not that easy. He especially likes to go to the back of the class and ask students questions, so bear in mind not to fall asleep in lecture otherwise you will be embarrassed.

Lectures are essential! Lectures are recorded and slides are posted.  Cliff is very informative and interesting, but his assessment is difficult.  Helpful to have a good TA, but you can also see Cliff for help too.

Burgess has a dry sense of humor and I personally find him boring. I took this class thinking it would be easy and interesting but I stand corrected. I found the midterms and final exam to be quite difficult considering this is only a first year class. Overall, I feel that I didn't get much out of this class. Boring material and boring prof.

disagree with the previous  comment, his final was fair,attend lectures&review the midterms, final is not that hard.you can tell he is really knowledgeable and passionate about what he's teaching. very helpful at office hour, doesn't just tell you the answer but rather teach you the process of solving it.has a different sense of humor, i like him.
very helpful prof，clear contents during the lecture，however the final is hard.
NEVER take his course. Teach 3xx level stuff in 2xx courses. tut madatory or receive -1 point. Mid-term extremely hard. Cant imagine how hard the final would be!
midterm is very hard. not recommend. every wek has assignment need to due and ppl who didnt do or complete the assignment will deduct 1% of participation mark. never take his class
Have no interest in discussing about the doubtful grading mistakes, grading system is kinda unfair.

One of the easiest electives I have taken @ SFU. Buy the textbook for practices, don't really have to read it because his notes already includes all the main points you'll need. The rest is just practice. You don't really have to study for final if you are on track throughout the semester. Easy A, hard to A+.

He is a great professor for this course.  I'd recommend him to teach LING 200.  But I have heard he makes LING 110 really hard.  He is passionate about this material, so it's good!  He has a lot of office hours, and if you've taken grammar in high school, the level of difficulty isn't too hard.  Just read the book, do the practices!

He is passionate about what he is teaching. Tutorials r useful. But his course is wayyyyyyyyy to hard. He assumes that Everyone loves linguistics as much as he does. Wake up! That is not possible.he is good, i personally like him. But i won't recommand my firends to take his course.unless you really llloooovveee linguistics.
85% spent going over homework, 10% waiting for people to answer homework questions, only 5% of the time spent actually teaching us the course material.
I would recommend the teacher but not the specific class. The textbook was extremely useful. Burgess is really good for having extra office hours before midterms and finals.
He is a helpful prof but not an easy course.
neat and useful slides.

Excellent prof. Straightforward, clear, and obviously interested in what he is teaching, but this course is VERY challenging. Between lectures and the textbook you should have a good understanding of the material but expect the exams to still be difficult. TA Rima's tutorials were not especially useful but the exercises required were.

Like other said, you do want to attend the lectures because he prepares really well and he is humurous! But exams are really demanding! Don't ever take it as an elective, cause you have to study it as a core course! Generally a really nice guy.
His lectures are pretty interesting, however his exams are really hard.  Be VERY prepared and study hard... otherwise, avoid this class.

Awesome prof! I took Linguistics as an elective out of curiosity and I totally do not regret it! Interesting lectures and lots of practice before exams. If you do your readings on time definitely an easy A!
Strongly NoT RECOMMEND to people take this courses as elective!! Midterm and final Exam are freaking hard !! He puted this 100 level course to 400 level!
very entertaining lectures but extremely difficult exams!2 midterms M/C and final M/C and definitions and short essay.not gpa booster for sure!

Super hard exam for both mid-term, and will be even harder on the final. Don't know if I could pass it or not. Interesting teaching skill, but he makes 100 level course like 400 one. Not a GPA booster. Don't take his course if you want to get a higher GPA.
Like his lecture,but very difficult midterms and he said the final won't be easy.Not doing curve.

Lectures are engaging and attentive. However, that's where his advantages end. His Exams are formed in a way that is extremely roundabout and ambiguous, have too many questions that are too specific and obscure, and he doesn't even talk about them in class at all! I don't recommend him as a GPA booster, I'm saying this after receiving an A-

Those who are whining about test difficulty and not adjusting teaching methods to "100 level students" should get a clue. His classes are not a walk in the park, and they shouldn't be. I've learned a lot through this prof - took 3 courses with him (Ling 100, 220 and 295). If you put in the effort to read and study, you'll do fine.
Took both LING 100 & 200 with him. Great Prof. Really passionate about what he is teaching. Love his lecture style, keeps me awake. Not so easy to get high marks though.

great prof, very professional, organized and funny, Tests are fair, but the fianl is bit tricky. NEGATIVE:answer your question in a round about way to let you think about it first.  Do study hard! If you are good at grammar, you are taking advantages of taking this course. it's just like math, you either get it or you don't!

I like cliff a lot! Notes r very clear so that u dont need to attend the lecture at all. He posted sample exams for all midterms and final which is extremely helpful. Kinda fun 2! dont know the final yet.But I will take other courses with him definitely.

Great prof. He knows his stuff and teaches with a passion. I came into the course with 0 interest but he made it fun every day. Very clear, helpful, and funny. VERY easy Midterm and Final.  GPA booster right here.

Horribly hard. The 100 level course felt like a 400 level due to his inability to adjust it to a younger generation (minus points for that. Also, 3 exams and not one paper??? How do you expect anyone to get higher than a C+??!!

He posts all of the notes online each week, and I honestly don`t find the point in going to class because all he does is go over his notes. You don`t even really need the textbook because his notes are very clear and concise. People complain that his midterms are hard, but I didn`t find them hard - they were fair, people just need to study better.

Burgess is an outstanding instructor. Two major components stand out. First, he has a background as an ESL instructor, which shines through in the clarity of his instruction. Second, he was once a lawyer, and his professionalism and high levels of organization are unlike anything I've encountered. If you take school seriously, take him.

Lectures can be quite boring.  He sends you notes every week so all you have to do is sit there in class and make sure that you understand what he is talking about.  If you can get someone from the previous semester to send you the questions from previous exams, that is the best, because they are pretty much all the same.  Just memorize everything.
It was a fair course, and I found it pretty interesting. He is a fun prof!

I've taken LING100 and 220 with him also. He's very easy to understand and he keeps you entertained in class. His exams aren't easy, but he's fair and only tests you on material that's covered in class. A fairly easy marker too. You should have no problem getting a B, B+, A- or A if you do the readings, study the material, and attend class.
hes super hard to follow during classes...ive even tried to listen to his recorded lectures and i totally fall asleep. he's hard to follow
pretends to be nice, extremely hard exams, he makes this course incredibly hard!!! lots of terminology to memorize, linguistics is not as easy as you think, just avoid this guy!
GOOD Prof...but it seems he taught too much. he conducts the class not like a 100 level course...students must think and think, then do better on his exam...the final is really hard though
well... Just don't be sad guys... Linguistics is not as easy as you think...

Although he is a nice guy, he expects too much from us. We take this course as electives, and I do not think anyone is going to major in linguistic in our class. He scaled the mid term and assignment 1, but its still not good enough. Since the tests and assign are hard. The class average is C and he just said that to us today in class.

I just hope that he scales the entire class because I'm not prepared to get anything below a B. I tried hard in this class, really hard. He's a great guy and I have hopes he won't let us down.. he has already scaled the midterm.

hard exams and hard assignments. Linguistic 220 is considered as the easiest elective..but do not think so for this one..probably do not take it with this prof...the class average overall is C+ after scaling. The other prof has the average around B...

The problem with Cliff is that he gives us really easy examples in the lecture and textbook, which are not helpful for his assignments and exams. The questions he gives are ambiguous, difficult, and he may use words we've never seen before. I heard everyone saying how easy Ling 220 is, but just don't see how it can be true.

Extremely hard midterm exam. Assignments are worth 15% each, and they are tricky, have only one week to finish. Class average for the midterm was 52% (before scaling).. overall class Average is around C+. Very pathetic, considering that this should be one of the easiest elective courses.

nice + funi guy...well prepared + organized...will help sort out any problems w/ the materials as long as u'll willing to ask...he's a tough, but fair marker...if u've been to his lectures then u'r already bound to pass...good teaching style...won't put u to sleep for sure~
lame jokes.even lamer exams

Great prof.  He might seem a little stand-offish in class, but is very approachable and gives so much help if you go and talk to him. Tests are difficult, but fair.  He gave study guides for all his lectures, so if you devote enough time to studying them, you'll be fine.
the best prof i've had at sfu. probably the best prof i've ever had in my post-secondary education. excellent lecturer, his notes are very helpful, and he's well-prepared for class.
He might be clear in his teaching and can communicate well but the exam is tough though and also he is not really friendly.
Great Prof, kept the class interesting. He's really good at explaining the material and does a lot of review so you know what's likely to end up on the exam. He made the class really enjoyable.
No Comments
No Comments
awesome, funny, and very interesting makes the students work hard for their grades
no comment

dr. burgess is a really responsible prof. he expects you to work hard, because he puts the same amount of time in terms of preparing for each class. his exams are fairly harder compare to exercises on the text book. he spends extra time going over problems with his students, very rare to find a prof. like that=)
No Comments
Very entertaining to listen to.
Great prof!

he's too stubborn to teach, always thinks he's right, and unwilling to discuss matters. on the second midterm he had a VERY ambiguous question.  he was unwilling to listen to my concerns and i ended up getting in a big argument with him in his office.

um...he is very responsible and serious for his teaching...so just be quiet in his class and do whatever he said to u~besides, dont try to argue with him...his tests r kinda fair...i got pretty high markd on MTs but Final~he really puts too much on final~so work hard!
total right! tries to b funny but not at all, quizzes/exams are completely diff. than what we did in class. final was brutal, i recommend NOT to take ANY courses with him, u'll regret, people!

Not a terrible class.  Scaled the marks, which is good.  He REALLY needs to drop the jokes though; I don't care what the person below said, but you don't have to be "smart" to get them - it's just that they're excruciatingly cheesy and pointless.

Exams were tough, but I still think the guy is an awesome prof and great person. I love his evil little jokes! Those that say he's not funny just aren't smart enough to get the jokes. He's really nice,got a great sense of humor, good teaching ability, and is now one of my favorite profs.
Tough exams, dry materials, it's bad to take his class unless you are familiar with his teaching style.
Not the best prof in the world.  Tough marking and dry material.
Jokes are lame and tests are lamer.  Ugh.

The guy himself - not bad.
 The guy as a prof - horrible.  If you are used to getting A's in courses that force you to actually think, don't bother with this mediocre elective.
I agree.  Horrible Tests.  NOT FUNNY.  Terrible and dry class overall.

Cute and shiny head.  ;).  Meh.  Really boring and bland.  Readings are useless and tests don't actually quiz you on what you know (and this is coming from someone who's getting a mark in the 90s right now).  Readings are unnecessary.  He's a good guy, but I would take a stats course over this one.
funny and cute:), makes u laugh through the lecture, and he`s the best in LING DEPT,. Lectures are clear and well-organized,nice guy!
Good Teaching style, clear, makes the course interesting to listen to. Most of the stuff on the tests are on the notes, but the tests are hard. The readings are unnecessary and very boring (dry).
He is a really interesting prof. His tests are not way to specific. The readings are kind of dry, but you can get away without reading them and still do well.

Nice Prof. funny guy...the midterms are fair, Textbooks are useless in this class= =!His notes are good enough to get a B+ on the tests, as long as you memorize thoes stuff on the notes..anyway, He's not bad!!
good prof quiz is a little bit hard ,but lecture is good.
Professor B. really helps you out if you show some initiative. And vervets really are important!
best prof. ever! well-organized. easy to follow. but little hard on quiz though... very helpful. interesting lecture. not boring even though three hours night class~
Rude and easily annoyed. class sucks and is boring as HELL. my pillow needed a pillow

Unfortunately, this class is a headache.  The assigned readings are dry, unnecessarily complicated and excessive.  Maybe Cliff should consider revising his expectations for the course (it's a first year course after all, right?  It's also an elective that is not REQUIRED for LING majors anyway).
He is very dynamic, class is always interesting

Well, seeing as this is a requirement for me, he's making the course BEARABLE.  He's funny at times and really not at others.  His tests are ruthless and complete "fluff": rather than testing our knowledge, he tests us on little irrelevant details.

Fantastic prof! Goes out of his way to help keen students, is very motivated & really loves what he teaches. Great course! I got an A on my first midterm, but I studied hard. Midterms straight out of the notes. He's funny & engaging & makes lectures interesting. Notes available on net before class!
Not helpful at all. Tries to be funny, but his arrogant style constanlty gets in his way. Doesn't like to be challenged intellectually. Part of SFU's old boys club establishment.
Very Articulated, tests are STRAIGHT out from notes.. Dont even bother with the readings
hard tests, funny class though.
no curbed marks. hard tests. fun class. If you dont care about marks take this class. If you do dont
i think he's a funny prof, sometimes will joke. his midterms and final exam are ok, not too difficult, i think. he reviews and does lots of applications to get u to know the material. not a bad prof.

Super hard, Quite cold and unhelpful, he doesn't answer question during his office hours but encourage us to go to his office hours, which is ironic. My tutor can explain things more clear than he does. Lectures are unorganized. Sometimes he is rude.
very hard, makes up stupid jokes dat arent funny at all. exams are ridiculous. he doesnt even know how to do the exams
boo-urns, avoid his classes at all costs

Very picky and easily irritated. VERY hard marker - first assignment he lowers your score by half by subtracting your wrong answers to your right answers and the whole semester you're second guessing whether or not your know what you're doing.

Worst professor I've ever had!  Aside from the barrage of horrible jokes that will leave you numb, his teaching style is extremely unhelpful.  Oh, and he's an arrogant ****, who refuses to admit when's he wrong.  Rapidly becoming senile.
super hard; even the students major in ling cant do his ling 110 hw; no notes in class at all; pretend to be funny, but not funny at all!!!
Great teaching.Good professor.It will be easier if he put up a lot of practice on the web.If u pay attention to everything he says,u r in good shape.
He is an interesting prodfessor, but be aware of the exams. Interesting lecture doesn't mean you have easy papers!!
midterms are fair but his final is nothing like the midterms and will eat you alive.he cracks stupid jokes.
Interesting lectures. Not very helpful once the show is over.
don't waste $ on the required text books. he's usually pretty clear when going over his notes in class.

Very good use of media in his course. Usage of website, tape recordings, and such are very helpful for 1st-year students. Enjoyable and interesting material. I personally am delighted with his effort to make light of his course.
provides detailed (but poorly formatted) notes, cracks lame jokes that aren't funny or endearing, easy exams provided you study his notes, but don't waste your time and money on the "required" texts
Hard material, but he is so helpful.  Very student-friendly teaching style.  Remembers faces from a lecture hall of 100's.

Clear prof with decent notes. Quizzes fair, but on final some really tricky stuff we barely glanced at in class. He thinks he's always right, so if you disagree, it's VERY hard to get him to change his mind (or your mark!)

He cracks too many stupid jokes, and hes usually the only one laughing. He's got great notes, very good/clear weekly summary notes that he posts on his webpage. He knows his stuff really well. Not an easy marker though, his questions are demanding.
he is the worst prof that I ever ever have in my university life!!! Yah, you can *only* study notes for his midterms, but what's the point we buy the textbooks? Trying to sucking our money or what?

Not an easy course, not the mosy interesting lectures (for what should be pretty interesting subject matter). Not helpful when seeking help. But definately a nice, and friendly guy. Cracks a lot of stillborn jokes in class. Could/Should be a good prof.

Tries to be funny in class.It helps to keep you awake at 830 in the morning. Helpful ONLY when it's not near an assignment... he won't say anything (nothing close) about anything on the assignment.If you study and read in advance, then you should do ok.
he gives you notes but very confusing if you don't know what you are doing. Assignment are HARD, exams are average.
notes are understandable and easy to grasp but the tests and assignments are a whole different issue

nice prof, explains stuff quite well, knows his stuff, very enthusiastic, tries to entertain the students, but not very good at it.  Makes hard tests though, but easy if you study and go to lectures and pay attention. don't take his class if u want marks.

I'm surprised at the number of people in other courses who say he's hard.  His lectures are..kinda interesting, tries to be funny, and his exams are pretty easy.  Just follow his notes and go to lectures.
The lecture is okay and so are his notes, but then again his exams and assignments are hard.  Nice guy though.
NO Comment, Absolutely NO Comment!!!!!
He made the easy stuff HARD!!!!
Not an easy course, good prof but is just very hard
very hard prof. he tries to be funny
very enjoyable course if taken with this guy...but relatively much memory work invovled...
I think he's hard, but then again I am a slacker.
he's a funny man
No Comments
No Comments
No Comments
